item.title: Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections, but Tehran denies claim
item.description: Iran insists there are no plans for inspections of its bombed nuclear sites, but Trump says Tehran "fully and completely" agreed to let inspectors return.

item.title: What led to Starmer's resignation as U.K. prime minister and who will likely replace him
item.description: Keir Starmer announced he's resigning as U.K. prime minister less than two years after he led the Labour Party to a general election victory. Now, Andy Burnham has emerged as the frontrunner to replace him. CBS News' Inaya Iman explains what led to Starmer's resignation and why Burnham is likely to be the country's next prime minister.

item.title: Chico library shooting suspect apparently wanted Columbine-type massacre, police say
item.description: Two people were killed and a child was injured in a shooting inside a library in Chico on Monday, officials said.

item.title: Supreme Court rules ex-inmate can't sue prison officials for shaving dreadlocks
item.description: The Supreme Court rejected a former Louisiana inmate's effort to sue state prison officials after they shaved his dreadlocks in violation of his religious beliefs.

item.title: 10 years later, a majority of Britons say "Brexit" was a mistake
item.description: Most Britons say leaving the European Union was a mistake and they'd favor a new referendum, but politicians have little appetite to reopen the wound.

item.title: 40 drowning deaths reported in France as Europe swelters in heat wave
item.description: Millions in France are enduring extreme heat, with temperatures soaring and 40 drowning deaths reported since June 18.

item.title: Deadly heat wave scorches Europe
item.description: Record-breaking heat is slamming Europe. CBS News meteorologist Rob Marciano takes a look at the forecast.

item.title: Tehran rejects Trump's claim that Iran "completely agreed" to nuclear inspections
item.description: The Trump administration and Iran are providing conflicting messages on potential nuclear inspections as part of a future agreement. Meanwhile, negotiations between the U.S. and Iran could drift further apart over Israel's war against Hezbollah in Lebanon. CBS News' Aaron Navarro has more.

item.title: Which debts are forgiven when someone dies?
item.description: When a loved one dies, certain types of debts may disappear, but others may impact heirs and the estate.

item.title: Jim Parsons says he knew he wanted to be part of "Titaníque" when offered role of Rose's mom
item.description: Jim Parsons speaks about starring in the musical "Titaníque," which is a comedic retelling of the story of the "Titanic," from the perspective and songs of Celine Dion. Parsons talks about why he wanted to portray's Rose's mother, the show's impact on the LGBTQ+ community and the cast.

item.title: Lionel Messi makes history as all-time best World Cup scorer during Argentina's victory over Austria
item.description: Argentina's Lionel Messi broke a World Cup record during a match against Austria, becoming the tournament's top-scoring athlete as he racked up his 18th goal across his World Cup career. Former U.S. player Charlie Davies gives Messi his kudos and reacts to other top headlines from the world of soccer.

item.title: Andy Burnham rises as top pick to succeed Keir Starmer as prime minister
item.description: British lawmaker Andy Burnham is a favorite pick to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer following Starmer's resignation announcement. Author and journalist Ian Dunt has more context.

item.title: Trump blames vandals for Reflecting Pool problems without providing proof
item.description: Preparations are underway to drain the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., for repairs after a multi-million dollar renovation gone awry. President Trump is blaming vandals for an alleged 350-foot gash in the pool's blue lining. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe has more on this.

item.title: U.S. commute times and distances are rising, new data shows
item.description: Average commute times and distances for the American worker are rising, now reaching lengths not seen since before the pandemic, new data shows. Business Insider senior labor and inequality reporter Juliana Kaplan explains.

item.title: Tech stocks tumble for a second day. Here's what is behind the selloff.
item.description: Nvidia, Alphabet and other technology stocks fell as Wall Street shifted from rewarding AI spending to demanding evidence that it will produce outsized returns.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: White House insists negotiations with Iran are progressing despite Israel-Lebanon tensions
item.description: President Trump and Vice President Vance are claiming that Iran agreed to let nuclear inspectors enter the country, but Tehran is denying those claims. This comes as fighting between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group in Lebanon threatens to derail U.S. negotiations with Iran. On Tuesday, teams from Israel and Lebanon will meet in Washington, D.C., to address the conflict. CBS News' Weijia Jiang has the latest.

item.title: Affordable and easy ways to make home safer for aging loved ones
item.description: The cost of home care and assisted living rose almost 50% from 2019 to 2024. Ash-har Quraishi shows simple steps to take to help make your loved one's home safer so they can stay in it longer.

item.title: Weighing the risks and benefits of GLP-1 drugs for kids struggling with obesity
item.description: Childhood and teen obesity rates have soared to record highs with about 1 in 5 living with obesity. The FDA has approved GLP-1 drugs for weight loss among kids 12 and older. Dr. Rachel Pessah-Pollack explains the risks and benefits, who may be a candidate and more.

item.title: World Cup "a complete game changer" for U.S. businesses as fans travel thousands of miles
item.description: The World Cup is expected to bring in $17 billion across the U.S. Kelly O'Grady spoke to international fans about their experiences and a business owner who says the event "is like nothing we've ever seen before."

item.title: Second ransom note in Nancy Guthrie case claims she died, sources say
item.description: Investigators familiar with the case believe it is likely that two ransom notes sent to Nancy Guthrie's family were written by the person or group that abducted her. While the first note demanded millions in bitcoin, the second claimed that Nancy Guthrie had died, albeit not purposefully, according to sources who reviewed the notes. CBS News' Bradley Blackburn has more details on the case.

item.title: Senate passes landmark housing affordability bill after bipartisan breakthrough
item.description: The Senate passed a bill aimed at lowering housing costs on Monday after a major breakthrough and rare bipartisan consensus.

item.title: India eyes a risky Everest mission to recover the body of "Green Boots"
item.description: A Mount Everest veteran tells CBS News why retrieving "Green Boots," whose remains have become a grim waypoint for climbers, would be a perilous mission.

item.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note, believed to be from abductor, said she died, sources say
item.description: Authorities believe two ransom notes addressed to Nancy Guthrie's family — including a note that said she had died — were likely sent by the person or group of people who abducted her.

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder suspect's ex-roommate can't testify at hearing, judge rules
item.description: The Utah judge in the murder case of Charlie Kirk's alleged killer has denied a defense request to force Tyler Robinson's former roommate to testify in person during the preliminary hearing.

item.title: AI on pace to bypass cybersecurity systems soon, "Five Eyes" spy partners warn
item.description: The most advanced artificial intelligence models are improving quickly enough to outsmart prevailing cybersecurity know-how within months, the Five Eyes spy agency alliance is warning.

item.title: Medicare's AI push snarls patients and doctors in errors and delays
item.description: Medicare is testing the use of artificial intelligence to preapprove several healthcare services.

item.title: Coke and Pepsi rolling out QR codes linked to ingredient information
item.description: The QR codes will take soda drinkers to a website listing more than 140 beverage ingredients and their nutritional content.

item.title: Polymarket launches probe after report alleges deceptive marketing
item.description: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that the prediction market paid content creators to produce videos of fake trades purporting to show big financial gains.

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the man likely to be Britain's next prime minister?
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is resigning, and the man widely expected to replace him is a fellow Labour Party lawmaker known as the "King of the North."
